tools.name: get_attachment
tools.description: Retrieve an attachment's content by ID.

tools.name: prepare_attachment_upload
tools.description:
Prepare a direct Linear file upload for an existing issue.

Workflow:
1. Call this tool with issue, filename, contentType, and size.
2. Upload raw bytes with PUT to uploadRequest.url outside MCP.
3. All headers in uploadRequest.headers are part of the signed request, so send them verbatim.
4. After PUT succeeds, call create_attachment_from_upload with assetUrl to link it to the issue.

Do not base64-encode or transform the file. Use curl --data-binary @path or fetch(url, { method: 'PUT', body: blob }).
Omitting or modifying any signed header, including casing, will return HTTP 403.
The signed URL must be used within 60 seconds or it will expire.

Example:
curl -X PUT --data-binary @file.png \
  -H "content-type: image/png" \
  -H "x-goog-content-length-range: N,N" \
  -H "cache-control: public, max-age=31536000" \
  -H 'Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="file.png"' \
  "<uploadRequest.url>"

filename.description: Filename for the upload, e.g. screenshot.png

contentType.description: MIME type, e.g. image/png or application/pdf

size.description: Exact file size in bytes. Must be smaller than 2 GB.

title.description: Suggested attachment title for the finalize step

subtitle.description: Suggested attachment subtitle for the finalize step

tools.name: create_attachment_from_upload
tools.description:
Link an already-uploaded Linear assetUrl to an existing issue as an attachment.

Use this only after:
1. prepare_attachment_upload returned an assetUrl and uploadRequest.
2. The client successfully PUT raw file bytes to uploadRequest.url.

This tool does not upload file content. It only creates the Linear attachment row.
If the direct upload failed or the signed URL expired, rerun prepare_attachment_upload and upload again.

assetUrl.description: Linear upload assetUrl returned by prepare_attachment_upload

title.description: Attachment title. Defaults to filename or asset URL

tools.name: create_attachment
tools.description: Deprecated fallback for tiny files only. Accepts base64 file content and uploads it through the MCP worker, which can consume large amounts of agent context. Prefer `prepare_attachment_upload` plus direct PUT plus `create_attachment_from_upload`.

base64Content.description: Deprecated base64-encoded file content to upload

tools.name: delete_attachment
tools.description: Delete an attachment by ID

tools.name: list_comments
tools.description: List comments on a Linear issue, project, initiative, document, or project milestone. Provide exactly one of `issueId`, `projectId`, `initiativeId`, `documentId`, or `milestoneId`. For issues, projects, and initiatives this returns both top-level discussion threads and inline description comments. Inline (anchored) comments carry a non-null `quotedText` set to the snippet of description text they reference.

issueId.description: Issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123) (provide exactly one parent)

projectId.description: Project name, ID, or slug (provide exactly one parent)

initiativeId.description: Initiative name or ID (provide exactly one parent)

documentId.description: Document ID or slug (provide exactly one parent)

milestoneId.description: Milestone UUID (provide exactly one parent). Resolve milestone names via `list_milestones` first.

tools.name: save_comment
tools.description: Create or update a comment on a Linear issue, project, initiative, document, or project milestone. If `id` is provided, updates the existing comment; otherwise creates a new one. To start a new thread, pass `body` and exactly one of `issueId`, `projectId`, `initiativeId`, `documentId`, or `milestoneId` — comments on issues/projects/initiatives become top-level discussion threads; comments on documents/milestones become description comments. To reply to an existing thread, pass `parentId` and `body`; the reply inherits the parent's thread type, so no entity reference is needed.

id.description: Comment ID. If provided, updates the existing comment

issueId.description: Issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123) (provide exactly one parent)

projectId.description: Project name, ID, or slug (provide exactly one parent)

initiativeId.description: Initiative name or ID (provide exactly one parent)

documentId.description: Document ID or slug (provide exactly one parent)

milestoneId.description: Milestone UUID (provide exactly one parent). Resolve milestone names via `list_milestones` first.

parentId.description: Parent comment ID (for replies, only when creating)

body.description: Content as Markdown. Do not escape the string — use literal newlines and special characters, not escape sequences. To mention a user, use @displayName (e.g., @johndoe)

tools.name: delete_comment
tools.description: Delete a Linear comment. Inline description comments (those with non-null `quotedText`) anchor a mark in the editor, so their root cannot be deleted — delete the replies individually or resolve the thread instead.

tools.name: list_cycles
tools.description: Retrieve cycles for a specific Linear team

type.description: Filter: current, previous, next, or all

tools.name: get_document
tools.description: Retrieve a Linear document by ID or slug

tools.name: list_documents
tools.description: List documents in the user's Linear workspace

tools.name: save_document
tools.description: Create or update a Linear document. If `id` is provided, updates the existing document; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `title` is required and exactly one parent (`project`, `issue`, `initiative`, or `cycle`) must be specified. On update, passing a parent reparents the document.

id.description: Document ID or slug to update. Omit to create a new document.

content.description: Content as Markdown. Do not escape the string — use literal newlines and special characters, not escape sequences. To mention a user, use @displayName (e.g., @johndoe)

cycle.description: Cycle name, number, or ID. When passing a name or number, also pass `team` to disambiguate.

team.description: Team name or ID. Only used to resolve `cycle`.

icon.description: Icon name or emoji code (e.g. "Rocket" or ":eagle:"), not a raw Unicode emoji

tools.name: extract_images
tools.description: Extract and fetch images from markdown content. Use this to view screenshots, diagrams, or other images embedded in Linear issues, comments, or documents. Pass the markdown content (e.g., issue description) and receive the images as viewable data.

markdown.description: Markdown content containing image references (e.g., issue description, comment body)

tools.name: get_issue
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about an issue by ID, including attachments and git branch name

tools.name: list_issues
tools.description: List issues in the user's Linear workspace. For my issues, use "me" as the assignee. Use "null" for no assignee.

delegate.description: Agent name or ID. When the user asks to delegate to "Linear" or "the Linear agent", this refers to the "Linear" app user specifically

parentId.description: Parent issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123)

tools.name: save_issue
tools.description: Create or update a Linear issue. If `id` is provided, updates the existing issue; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `title` and `team` are required. Note: use `assignee` (not `assigneeId`) to set the assignee — it accepts a user ID, name, email, or "me".

id.description: Only for updating an existing issue. Pass the issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123). Do NOT pass this parameter when creating a new issue.

description.description: Content as Markdown. Do not escape the string — use literal newlines and special characters, not escape sequences. To mention a user, use @displayName (e.g., @johndoe)

team.description: Team name or ID (required when creating)

cycle.description: Cycle name, number, or ID. Null to remove

assignee.description: User ID, name, email, or "me". Null to remove

delegate.description: Agent name or ID. When the user asks to delegate to "Linear" or "the Linear agent", this refers to the "Linear" app user specifically. Null to remove

parentId.description: Parent issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123). Null to remove

links.description: Link attachments to add [{url, title}]. Append-only; existing links are never removed

blocks.description: Issue IDs/identifiers this blocks. Append-only; existing relations are never removed

blockedBy.description: Issue IDs/identifiers blocking this. Append-only; existing relations are never removed

relatedTo.description: Related issue IDs/identifiers. Append-only; existing relations are never removed

duplicateOf.description: Duplicate of issue ID/identifier. Null to remove

removeBlockedBy.description: Issue IDs/identifiers to remove as blockers of this issue

tools.name: list_issue_statuses
tools.description: List available issue statuses in a Linear team

tools.name: get_issue_status
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about an issue status in Linear by name or ID

tools.name: list_issue_labels
tools.description: List available issue labels in a Linear workspace or team

tools.name: create_issue_label
tools.description: Create a new Linear issue label

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List projects in the user's Linear workspace

tools.name: get_project
tools.description: Retrieve details of a specific project in Linear

tools.name: save_project
tools.description: Create or update a Linear project. If `id` is provided, updates the existing project; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `name` and at least one team (via `addTeams` or `setTeams`) are required.

id.description: Project ID. If provided, updates the existing project

icon.description: Icon name or emoji code (e.g. "Rocket" or ":eagle:"), not a raw Unicode emoji

description.description: Content as Markdown. Do not escape the string — use literal newlines and special characters, not escape sequences. To mention a user, use @displayName (e.g., @johndoe)

startDate.description: Start date (ISO format). Pair with startDateResolution to indicate precision (e.g. month, quarter)

targetDate.description: Target date (ISO format). Pair with targetDateResolution to indicate precision (e.g. month, quarter)

addTeams.description: Team name or ID to add

removeTeams.description: Team name or ID to remove

setTeams.description: Replace all teams with these. Cannot combine with addTeams/removeTeams

lead.description: User ID, name, email, or "me". Null to remove

setInitiatives.description: Replace all initiatives with these. Cannot combine with addInitiatives/removeInitiatives

tools.name: list_project_labels
tools.description: List available project labels in the Linear workspace

tools.name: get_diff
tools.description: Exact lookup for a Linear diff. Use with review URLs, GitHub PR URLs, Linear full identifiers, UUIDs, or slugs.

urlOrId.description: Linear review URL, diff slug, pull request ID, Linear full identifier, or GitHub PR URL

tools.name: list_diffs
tools.description: List Linear diff pull requests visible to the authenticated user

query.description: Broad search by title, branch, PR number, or bare slug

owner.description: Filter returned diffs by repository owner

repo.description: Filter returned diffs by repository name

status.description: Filter returned diffs by pull request status

tools.name: get_diff_threads
tools.description: Exact lookup for diff threads. Use with review URLs, GitHub PR URLs, Linear full identifiers, UUIDs, or slugs.

urlOrId.description: Linear review URL, diff slug, pull request ID, Linear full identifier, or GitHub PR URL

threadId.description: Optional top-level thread/comment ID to return

resolved.description: Filter returned threads by resolved state

tools.name: list_milestones
tools.description: List all milestones in a Linear project

tools.name: get_milestone
tools.description: Retrieve details of a specific milestone by ID or name

tools.name: save_milestone
tools.description: Create or update a milestone in a Linear project. If `id` is provided, updates the existing milestone; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `name` is required.

targetDate.description: Target completion date (ISO format, null to remove)

tools.name: list_teams
tools.description: List teams in the user's Linear workspace

tools.name: get_team
tools.description: Retrieve details of a specific Linear team

tools.name: list_users
tools.description: Retrieve users in the Linear workspace

tools.name: get_user
tools.description: Retrieve details of a specific Linear user

tools.name: search_documentation
tools.description: Search Linear's documentation to learn about features and usage
